title: 怪钟疑案
summary:
Inspector Hakaste (also referred to as Detective Hercaster, Hardcastle, or Hacker) conducts several interviews regarding the murder of a nineteen-year-old stenographer found in a telephone booth. Following an interrogation of Miss Pembroke, who denies seeing the girl or any suspicious activity, the Inspector visits Miss Wutherspoon (also referred to as Miss Walters, Miss Watkins, or Miss Whitehouse). 

Miss Wutherspoon claims she discovered the girl after attempting to use a phone booth because her own phone was making a strange buzzing noise. When questioned about reports of the girl visiting her front gate, she suggests the girl might have merely been delivering a flyer. She then suggests the girl may have actually been visiting Miss Pembury, noting that a stenographer had reportedly visited Miss Pembury on the day a man was killed. The Inspector suspects Miss Wutherspoon may be attempting to deflect suspicion by redirecting the investigation toward No. 19.

The Inspector then visits the Cavendish Typewriting Agency to see Miss Sheila Webber. He is met by Miss Martin (also referred to as Miss Martindeer or Miss Martindale), who berates him for the lack of police protection. She argues that a person with a "perverted psychology" is targeting shorthand typists, noting that Miss Webber was previously traumatized by being sent to discover a corpse, and now a girl named Iena has been murdered. Miss Martin expresses concern that the killings are part of a "mad scheme" and mentions rumors regarding clocks, which the Inspector dismisses as a matter of trial postponement.
